channel.title: IT-Security & Privacy News Aggregator
channel.description: Aggregierter RSS-Feed aus führenden IT-Security, Privacy und Cyber Policy Quellen

item.title: Local Police Collusion Hampers Crackdown on Asian Scam Centers
item.description: With tens of billions of dollars flowing into regional economies from cybercrime, scam centers continue to flourish, despite international and law-enforcement efforts.
item.dc:creator: Robert Lemos

item.title: AI and Liability
item.description: <p>Earlier this month, a German court <a href="https://the-decoder.com/landmark-german-ruling-declares-googles-ai-overviews-are-googles-own-words-and-makes-it-liable-for-false-answers/">ruled</a> that Google is liable for its AI search summaries. Rejecting defenses like &#8220;users can check for themselves,&#8221; and that they generally know &#8220;that information generated with AI should not be blindly trusted,&#8221; the court held that the AI&#8217;s summaries are reflections of the company and &#8220;above all an expression of Google&#8217;s business activities.&#8221;</p> <p>This is the latest skirmish in a decades-old battle over internet publishing. Historically, there were two different types of information distributors: carriers and publishers. A phone company is a carrier. It&#8217;ll transmit whatever you say, even discussions about committing a crime. Words are words, and the phone company does not know&#8212;nor is it liable for&#8212;the words you choose to speak. A newspaper, on the other hand, is a publisher. It decides the words it publishes, and what quotes to include in its articles. If those words or quotes are defamatory or otherwise illegal, it&#8217;s liable...</p>
item.dc:creator: Bruce Schneier

item.title: Interpol: Cybercrime Hits 30% of Recorded Crime in Surveyed APAC Countries
item.description: <p>Interpol’s latest Asia and South Pacific cybercrime assessment shows how phishing, ransomware, DDoS attacks, infostealers, and AI-enabled scams are raising security risks across APAC.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com/article/news-interpol-cybercrime-report-apac/">Interpol: Cybercrime Hits 30% of Recorded Crime in Surveyed APAC Countries</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com">TechRepublic</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: TechRepublic Staff

item.title: Cisco Vulnerability Exploited Months Before Disclosure, Google Warns
item.description: A high-severity flaw in Cisco Catalyst SD-WAN Manager disclosed in early June was exploited as early as March

item.title: Chrome Ad Blocker with 10M+ Installs Found with Dormant Script Injection Capability
item.description:
An analysis of a popular Google Chrome ad block extension for YouTube has uncovered the ability to execute arbitrary JavaScript code.
According to Island, the extension, named Adblock for YouTube (ID: cmedhionkhpnakcndndgjdbohmhepckk), has more than 10 million installs and carries a Featured badge on the Chrome Web Store.
The extension description states that it allows users to prevent web
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Europol, Microsoft Hit Malware Network Behind 27M Stolen Logins, 140,000 Infected Computers
item.description: <p>Europol and Microsoft disrupted malware infrastructure linked to 27 million stolen login credentials and 140,000 infected computers in a global cybercrime network.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com/article/news-europol-microsoft-malware-takedown-emea-eu/">Europol, Microsoft Hit Malware Network Behind 27M Stolen Logins, 140,000 Infected Computers</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com">TechRepublic</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Liz Ticong

item.title: Twenty Million US IP Connections Used by Proxy Services
item.description: Digital Citizens Alliance report claims that millions of Americans may have unwittingly had IP connections used by cybercriminals

item.title: Runlayer Raises $30 Million in Series A Funding
item.description: <p>The startup’s platform functions as a secure control layer, aiming to secure AI tools across enterprises.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.securityweek.com/runlayer-raises-30-million-in-series-a-funding/">Runlayer Raises $30 Million in Series A Funding</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.securityweek.com">SecurityWeek</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Ionut Arghire

item.title: ThreatsDay Bulletin: Smart TV Proxyware, 24-Year curl Bug, AI Crime Forums + 13 More Stories
item.description:
It’s dumb out there again.
This week has the usual smell of prod on fire and nobody wanting to admit who left the door open — old creds still working, trusted apps doing sketchy crap, browser tricks jumping the fence, and “normal” workflows turning into phishing pipes because apparently email was not enough hell already.
The worst part is how cheap some of it feels. Not elite. Not cinematic.
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Cal Water Says No OT Systems Breached in Iranian Handala Cyberattack
item.description: <p>Mandiant has helped the California water utility investigate the cyberattack launched by Iranian hacker group Handala.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.securityweek.com/cal-water-finds-no-evidence-of-ot-activity-after-hackers-claimed-they-could-disrupt-water-supply/">Cal Water Says No OT Systems Breached in Iranian Handala Cyberattack</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.securityweek.com">SecurityWeek</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Eduard Kovacs

item.title: Trust in Automated AI Vulnerability Scanning Collapses to 9%, New Study Finds
item.description: Cobalt study finds 20-percentage-point drop in number of organizations relying solely on AI automation for testing

item.title: New CISA Guide Helps Agencies Adopt SASE For Zero Trust
item.description: New CISA guidance shows federal agencies how to use SASE to move from legacy TIC 2.0 to zero trust

item.title: Interesting Paper Exploring Prompt Injection
item.dc:creator: Bruce Schneier

item.title: Lantronix Serial-to-IP Converter Flaw Exploited in Attacks After OT Threat Warning
item.description: <p>The exploited flaw, CVE-2025-67038, is one of the vulnerabilities disclosed in April as part of the BRIDGE:BREAK research project.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.securityweek.com/lantronix-serial-to-ip-converter-flaw-exploited-in-attacks-after-ot-threat-warning/">Lantronix Serial-to-IP Converter Flaw Exploited in Attacks After OT Threat Warning</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.securityweek.com">SecurityWeek</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Eduard Kovacs

item.title: Surviving the Mythos Era: Richard Bejtlich on the Case for NDR
item.description:
Despite the abundance of telemetry at analysts’ disposal, many security operations teams struggle to answer a few basic questions during incident investigation: What happened? What evidence do we have? How do we know we’re seeing it all, in context?
Answering these questions requires teams to go beyond alerts, the most common basis for initial triage. But investigations (and their outcomes)
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: GitLab Patches Code Execution, Information Disclosure Vulnerabilities
item.description: <p>The latest GitLab CE/EE updates address 13 vulnerabilities, including three high-severity defects.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.securityweek.com/gitlab-patches-code-execution-information-disclosure-vulnerabilities/">GitLab Patches Code Execution, Information Disclosure Vulnerabilities</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.securityweek.com">SecurityWeek</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Ionut Arghire

item.title: macOS Flaw Lets Standard Users Disable EDR and MDM
item.description: macos-xpc-flaw-disable-edr-mdm-standard-user-xm-cyber

item.title: Major Increase in Ransomware Attacks Targeting Europe, Warns New Report
item.description: Analysis of ransomware incidents by researchers at Black Kite found that attacks have risen by over 50% in the last year, with supply chain attacks increasing

item.title: Europe Evolves Into Ransomware's Favorite Region
item.description: After a global lull, ransomware gangs are setting sights on a rich new arena: attacking EU organizations and their suppliers.
item.dc:creator: Nate Nelson

item.title: 25-Year-Old Vulnerability Patched in Curl
item.description: <p>The latest version of the open source data transfer tool resolves 18 medium and low-severity vulnerabilities.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.securityweek.com/25-year-old-vulnerability-patched-in-curl/">25-Year-Old Vulnerability Patched in Curl</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.securityweek.com">SecurityWeek</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Ionut Arghire

item.title: New Gaslight macOS Malware Uses Prompt Injection to Disrupt AI-Assisted Analysis
item.description:
A previously undocumented Rust-based macOS implant and information stealer has been found to embed a prompt injection payload designed to trick a malware analyst's artificial intelligence (AI) tools and trick it into aborting or refusing an analysis of the artifact.
The malware has been codenamed Gaslight owing to this deceptive behavior. It's been assessed with high confidence that the tool is
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: SecurityWeek ICS Cybersecurity Conference Heads to Nashville for Special 25-Year Anniversary Edition
item.description: <p>The 2026 Industrial Control Systems (ICS) Cybersecurity Conference takes place October 6-8, 2026, at the W Nashville.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.securityweek.com/securityweek-ics-cybersecurity-conference-heads-to-nashville-for-special-25-year-anniversary-edition/">SecurityWeek ICS Cybersecurity Conference Heads to Nashville for Special 25-Year Anniversary Edition</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.securityweek.com">SecurityWeek</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Mike Lennon

item.title: New Mistic Backdoor Linked to KongTuke in ClickFix and ModeloRAT Campaigns
item.description:
A new, stealthy backdoor named Mistic has been deployed as part of suspected financially motivated attacks aimed at multiple organizations spanning insurance, education, IT, and professional services sectors since April 2026.
According to Symantec and Carbon Black's Threat Hunter Team, the backdoor, also tracked as MLTBackdoor, is said to be linked to an initial access broker (IAB) named
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: NIST Opens Updated IoT Security Guidance to Public Review
item.description: <p>The guidance aims to establish product cybersecurity requirements for IoT devices integrated into federal agencies’ networks.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.securityweek.com/nist-opens-updated-iot-security-guidance-to-public-review/">NIST Opens Updated IoT Security Guidance to Public Review</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.securityweek.com">SecurityWeek</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Ionut Arghire

item.title: Chrome 149 Update Resolves 18 Severe Vulnerabilities
item.description: <p>More than half of the bugs are use-after-free defects, which can potentially lead to remote code execution.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.securityweek.com/chrome-149-update-resolves-18-severe-vulnerabilities/">Chrome 149 Update Resolves 18 Severe Vulnerabilities</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.securityweek.com">SecurityWeek</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Ionut Arghire

item.title: Cisco SD-WAN Zero-Day Exploited Months Before Patching
item.description: <p>CVE-2026-20245, the 7th Cisco SD-WAN vulnerability exploited in 2026, was used for months prior to its disclosure and patching.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.securityweek.com/cisco-sd-wan-zero-day-exploited-months-before-patching/">Cisco SD-WAN Zero-Day Exploited Months Before Patching</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.securityweek.com">SecurityWeek</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Eduard Kovacs

item.title: Cisco Catalyst SD-WAN Zero-Day CVE-2026-20245 Exploited to Gain Root Access
item.description:
An unknown threat actor exploited a recently disclosed high-severity security flaw impacting Cisco Catalyst SD-WAN as a zero-day at least two months before it was publicly disclosed, according to new findings from Google-owned Mandiant.
The vulnerability, tracked as CVE-2026-20245 (CVSS score: 7.8), allows an authenticated, local attacker to execute arbitrary commands with elevated privileges
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Attackers Hit Cisco SD-WAN Flaw 2 Months Before Disclosure
item.description: Researchers believe rogue peering was used to connect to the victim's SD-WAN devices to gain admin privileges and root-level access.
item.dc:creator: Jai Vijayan

item.title: 2026 FIFA World Cup Faces Surge in Cyber Threats
item.description: Persistent cybercrime, social engineering, and infrastructure threats continue to plague the FIFA 2026 World Cup across the US, Canada, and Mexico.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Culafi

item.title: Do CISOs Need a Code of Ethics?
item.description: Dark Reading Confidential Episode 19: Kickbacks, no-show jobs, &quot;dirty&quot; VCs, and shelf ware — industry expert Robert &quot;RSnake&quot; Hansen explains why he thinks it's time for a CISO code of ethics. It could ensure cybersecurity bosses aren't engaged in self-dealing that could risk enterprise, and even national, security.
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading Editorial Team

item.title: Healthcare Vendor Xsolis Reports Breach Affecting 1.4M People
item.description: <p>Xsolis confirmed a healthcare data breach affecting nearly 1.4 million people after a phishing attack exposed health and identity data.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com/article/news-xsolis-healthcare-data-breach/">Healthcare Vendor Xsolis Reports Breach Affecting 1.4M People</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com">TechRepublic</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Liz Ticong

item.title: Apple’s £3B iCloud Lawsuit Could Affect 40M UK Users
item.description: <p>Apple lost a bid to narrow a UK iCloud lawsuit from Which?, keeping a £3 billion competition claim on track for an October 2028 trial.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com/article/news-emea-uk-apple-icloud-lawsuit-2028-trial/">Apple’s £3B iCloud Lawsuit Could Affect 40M UK Users</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com">TechRepublic</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Joseph Ofonagoro

item.title: LastPass Confirms Vendor Breach Exposed Customer Contact, Support Data
item.description: <p>LastPass said customer contact and support data were exposed after attackers used stolen Klue OAuth tokens to access its Salesforce environment and CRM records.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com/article/news-lastpass-klue-oauth-token-salesforce-data-exposure/">LastPass Confirms Vendor Breach Exposed Customer Contact, Support Data</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com">TechRepublic</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Aminu Abdullahi

item.title: When Information Becomes the Attack Surface – Understanding AI Agent Traps
item.description: <p>From hidden content injections to cognitive state poisoning, attackers are turning trusted data sources into traps for autonomous AI.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.securityweek.com/when-information-becomes-the-attack-surface-understanding-ai-agent-traps/">When Information Becomes the Attack Surface &#8211; Understanding AI Agent Traps</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.securityweek.com">SecurityWeek</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Etay Maor

item.title: CISA Warns Critical Lantronix EDS5000 Flaw Is Being Actively Exploited
item.description:
The U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) on Tuesday warned of active exploitation of a critical security flaw impacting Lantronix EDS5000 Series devices, urging Federal Civilian Executive Branch (FCEB) agencies to apply the fixes by June 26, 2026.
The vulnerability in question is CVE-2025-67038 (CVSS score: 9.8), a code injection flaw that could result in the execution
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: More Malicious OpenClaw Skills Threaten AI Supply Chain
item.description: OpenClaw removed five packages from its ClawHub skills marketplace that bypassed security checks even though they included infostealers and other threats.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Montalbano

item.title: Researchers Trick AI Browsers Into Leaking Credentials
item.description: LayerX tricked AI browsers including ChatGPT Atlas and Comet into bypassing their guardrails

item.title: Amadey and StealC Malware Network Disrupted, 27M Stolen Credentials Recovered
item.description:
A coordinated law enforcement operation, in partnership with private sector companies, including Bitdefender, Bitsight, ESET, and Microsoft, has resulted in the takedown of criminal infrastructure powering Amadey and StealC.
"The main common goal was to disrupt the 'assembly lines' cybercriminals use to launch ransomware, financial fraud, and attacks on critical infrastructure," Europol said in
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Anthropic Launches Claude Tag, Bringing AI Agents Into Slack
item.description: <p>Anthropic launched Claude Tag in Slack, giving enterprise teams an AI agent with shared context, admin controls, logs, and spend limits.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com/article/news-anthropic-claude-tag-ai-agent-slack/">Anthropic Launches Claude Tag, Bringing AI Agents Into Slack</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com">TechRepublic</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Kezia Jungco

item.title: Europol-Led Operation Endgame Takes Down StealC and Amadey Infostealers
item.description: Operation Endgame seized around 50 domains and nearly 200 active IP-based servers associated with the infostealers

item.title: Madison Square Garden Hack Exposes 26 Million Visitor Records
item.description: <p>Madison Square Garden faces a 26M-record hack tied to visitor data, facial recognition, and security records from its venue operations, with fallout from the leak. </p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com/article/news-madison-square-garden-hack-26m-records/">Madison Square Garden Hack Exposes 26 Million Visitor Records</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com">TechRepublic</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Liz Ticong

item.title: macOS Backdoor Uses Prompt Injection to Evade AI Triage
item.description: SentinelLabs found a North Korea-linked macOS backdoor using prompt injection on AI triage tools

item.title: Coffee with the Council Podcast: Meet This Year’s North America Community Meeting Keynote Speaker, Sharon Gai
item.dc:creator: amalone@pcisecuritystandards.org (Alicia Malone)

item.title: Cordyceps CI/CD Flaws Expose 300+ GitHub Repositories to Supply-Chain Attacks
item.description:
Cybersecurity researchers have flagged a new class of CI/CD workflow weakness that allows attackers to hijack workflows and compromise open-source supply chains.
The "critical exploitable pattern" has been codenamed Cordyceps by Novee Security. The issue can allow full attacker control of repositories at dozens of the largest organizations worldwide, including Microsoft, Google, Apache, and
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: KDDI Breach Affects Six Japanese ISPs, Exposes 14.2 Email Credentials
item.description: Customers of the affected Japanese email services are “strongly advised” to change their email passwords

item.title: ESET takes part in Operation Endgame to disrupt Amadey and Stealc
item.description: ESET researchers assisted in the global disruption of the Amadey botnet and Stealc infostealer, providing technical analysis, infrastructure tracking, and affiliate-level insights

item.title: Apple's MacOS Gap Lets Users Disable Security Tools
item.description: Attackers can exploit the issue to disable security and integrated browser tools without needing administrator privileges or kernel exploits.
item.dc:creator: Jai Vijayan

item.title: Iran-Linked MuddyWater Poses as Ransomware Gang to Mask Cyber Espionage
item.description: An NCC Group report warns state-backed hackers are attempting to hide activity by posing as ransomware groups and deploying commercially available malware

item.title: Advancing Product Security: New IoT Guidance and New Engagement
item.description: It may be summertime, but the NIST Cybersecurity for the Internet of Things (IoT) Program isn’t hitting the hammock! Organizations are managing growing device complexity, evolving threats, and pressure to turn guidance into operational decisions…so we remain focused on helping stakeholders apply security guidance in ways that are practical and actionable. What’s Been Happening Lately? An initial public draft (IPD) of NIST SP 800-213 Revision 1, IoT Product Cybersecurity Guidelines for the Federal Government: Establishing IoT Product Cybersecurity Requirements is out now for your review and
item.dc:creator: Michael Fagan

item.title: Dawn of the Apex Agentic Adversary
item.description:
We are standing at the end of an era we never thought to mourn: the era of human-speed threats.
For years, cybersecurity moved to a rhythm organizations could follow. A researcher found a bug, a CVE was cataloged, a vendor navigated a patch cycle, and weeks or even months later, a fix was deployed. In this era, dwell time was measured in days, sometimes weeks. We are now approaching an
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Embedding Forbidden Text in Spyware to Discourage AI Analysis
item.dc:creator: Bruce Schneier

item.title: AI Is Making Attacks Cheaper, Faster and More Covert, Says ReliaQuest
item.description: New ReliaQuest study reveals the six ways AI is practically being used in attacks today

item.title: DoJ Seizes Huione Cloud Account Tied to Cyber Scam Money Laundering
item.description:
The U.S. Department of Justice (DoJ) on Tuesday announced the seizure of a cloud computing account put to use by subsidiaries of Cambodia-based corporate conglomerate HuiOne Group, as the Treasury unveiled fresh sanctions against nine individuals and 26 entities linked to Prince Group.
"These subsidiaries are alleged to have assisted individuals and organizations in transferring proceeds of
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: UK Museums Face Cybersecurity Risks, MPs Warn
item.description: Public Accounts Committee (PAC) warns that museums and galleries aren’t getting enough government support on cyber

item.title: How to Answer a Security Questionnaire: A 4-Step Guide | UpGuard
item.description: Learn best practices on how to answer a security questionnaire as a vendor.

item.title: What is a Security Questionnaire? | UpGuard
item.description: A security questionnaire helps organizations identify potential cybersecurity weaknesses among their third-party and even fourth-party vendors. Learn more.

item.title: What Is an RFP Response? A Guide for Security and GRC Teams | UpGuard
item.description: Stop letting the RFP security section bottleneck your sales. Learn how GRC teams can streamline response workflows, map controls, and close deals faster

item.title: What Is Sales Enablement? A Guide for Security and Compliance Teams | UpGuard
item.description: Discover why security reviews are the hidden sales enablement bottleneck, and learn a strategy for overcoming this hurdle to accelerate deal velocity.

item.title: Cisco Unified CM Flaw Exploited After PoC Reveals File-Write Path to Root
item.description:
Threat actors have begun to exploit a recently disclosed critical security flaw impacting Cisco Unified Communications Manager (Unified CM) and Unified Communications Manager Session Management Edition (Unified CM SME).
The vulnerability, tracked as CVE-2026-20230 (CVSS score: 8.6), is a case of improper input validation for specific HTTP requests that could allow an unauthenticated, remote
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Vendor Risk Assessments: An Ultimate Guide | UpGuard
item.description: Master vendor risk assessment: frameworks, real examples, and tools. Learn how automation and continuous monitoring strengthen your security.

item.title: Implementing A Vendor Risk Assessment Process in 2026 | UpGuard
item.description: A framework for implementing an efficient vendor risk assessment workflow into a Vendor Risk Management program in 2026.

item.title: Vendor Risk Management Checklist (Updated 2026) | UpGuard
item.description: This is a complete vendor risk management checklist. Learn how to reduce your third and fourth-party risk with this in-depth post.

item.title: Vendor Due Diligence Questionnaire (Free Template) | UpGuard
item.description: Learn all about vendor due diligence questionnaires, and customize one for your organization using our free VDDQ template.

item.title: Tata Electronics Leak Exposes 200,000 Files, Including Apple and Tesla Documents
item.description: <p>Tata Electronics is investigating a cyber incident after leaked files reportedly included manufacturing documents for Apple and Tesla.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com/article/news-apac-india-tata-electronics-data-leak/">Tata Electronics Leak Exposes 200,000 Files, Including Apple and Tesla Documents</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com">TechRepublic</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Joseph Ofonagoro

item.title: Scope of Salesforce Attacks Expands as Icarus Leaks Data
item.description: More victims have emerged after attackers breached application vendor Klue and used its OAuth tokens to steal customers' Salesforce data.
item.dc:creator: Rob Wright

item.title: 'Cordyceps': Mushrooming Malicious Pull Requests Threaten Developer Workflows
item.description: The CI/CD workflow weakness affects Microsoft's Azure Sentinel, Google's AI Agent Development Kit, Apache's Doris analytics database, Cloudflare's Workers SDK, and Python Software Foundation's Black.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Culafi

item.title: FortiBleed Targeted FortiGate Firewalls in 110 Million-Credential Harvesting Operation
item.description:
A Russian-speaking initial access broker (IAB) driven by financial gain is assessed to be behind a large-scale credential-harvesting operation known as FortiBleed that has targeted over 430,000 FortiGate firewalls globally.
The campaign, active since February 2026, involves collecting credential lists, searching for exposed services, brute-forcing accessible systems, and deploying bespoke
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Scattered Spider Hackers Plead Guilty on Day 1 of Trial
item.description: Two men pleaded guilty in the United Kingdom this week to criminal charges stemming from an August 2024 cyberattack that crippled Transport for London, the entity responsible for the public transport network in the Greater London area. The duo were key members of a prolific cybercrime group known as Scattered Spider, and their guilty pleas came on the first day of what was expected to be a six-week trial.
item.dc:creator: BrianKrebs

item.title: Fake AI Agent Skill Passed Security Scans and Reportedly Reached 26,000 Agents
item.description:
Security firm&nbsp;AIR&nbsp;built a fake AI agent skill, pushed it through a popular skill marketplace and an Instagram ad, and says it reached roughly 26,000 agents, including some on corporate accounts.
Every skill security scanner the firm tested it against marked it safe. The payload was harmless by design: it collected the user's email address and did nothing else.
The point was to show
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Trump Order Sets 2030 Deadline for Federal Post-Quantum Crypto Migration
item.description:
President Trump signed an&nbsp;executive order on June 22&nbsp;setting hard deadlines for federal agencies to move high-value assets and high-impact systems to post-quantum cryptography.
Key establishment must move by December 31, 2030; digital signatures by December 31, 2031. EO 14409 leaves national security systems on a separate track.
The deadlines matter because of a threat that does not
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Lookalike npm Package Hides a Multi-Stage Windows RAT
item.description: JFrog found an npm package impersonating postcss-selector-parser to drop a multi-stage Windows RAT

item.title: GitHub Updates actions/checkout to Block Common Pwn Request Attack Patterns
item.description:
GitHub is moving to strengthen software supply chain security by updating "actions/checkout" to block pwn request attacks that exploit the risky use of the "pull_request_target workflow" trigger to run malicious code with the workflow's full privileges.
Effective June 18, 2026, the latest version of "actions/checkout," the official GitHub action for checking out a repository into the
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: OpenAI Expands Daybreak to Help Defenders Patch Flaws
item.description: OpenAI expanded Daybreak with a full GPT-5.5-Cyber release to help defenders patch software flaws

item.title: Trump Issues Executive Order to Fast-Track Post-Quantum Migration
item.description: All US federal agencies will have to complete their post-quantum cryptography transition by 2031, according to a new Trump Executive Order

item.title: SocGholish Takedown Highlights Malicious TDS Threats
item.description: SocGholish uses traffic distribution systems (TDSs) to provide initial access into victims' networks for cybercrime groups such as the notorious Evil Corp.
item.dc:creator: Rob Wright

item.title: Best Data Breach and Data Leak Detection Tools in 2026 | UpGuard
item.description: Stopping an intrusion is half the battle. Our 2026 guide ranks the top breach and leak tools to neutralize exposed credentials before they are weaponized.

item.title: GTA 6 Scams Emerge as Pre-Orders Open
item.description: Cybercriminals launch fake GTA 6 pre-order sites offering early access for crypto payments

item.title: FortiBleed Attackers Turn Firewalls Into Credential Stealers as Heists Persist
item.description: The threat actors engineered a Golang-based sniffer to target 430,000 FortiGate firewalls and identify 110 million credentials in the ongoing global campaign.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Montalbano

item.title: Agentic AI: The Weapon That No Longer Needs a Warrior
item.description: Every weapon begins as an extension of the hand that holds it. The spear lengthened the reach of the arm. The bow sent the point flying without the throw. The rifle placed a man's death a quarter mile beyond his sight, and the aircraft carried that death across oceans. At each turn, the distance between the warrior and the wound grew wider, and yet one thing never moved: a human chose the target
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Anthropic’s Fable 5 Model Jailbroken Within Days
item.description: <p>Fable 5 is the supposed safe version of Anthropic&#8217;s Mythos Preview, with guardrails to ensure that it can&#8217;t be used to create cyberattacks.</p> <p>Well, that restriction was <a href="https://cybersecuritynews.com/anthropics-claude-fable-5-jailbroken/">bypassed</a> within days.</p>
item.dc:creator: Bruce Schneier

item.title: Best Vulnerability Management Tools and Software in 2026 | UpGuard
item.description: Compare the top vulnerability management software (VMS) platforms of 2026. This comprehensive guide reviews industry leaders like UpGuard, Tenable, Qualys,

item.title: Scattered Spider Teens Convicted of TfL Cyber-Attack
item.description: Two young British men have pleaded guilty to hacking Transport for London as part of a Scattered Spider plot

item.title: Malicious npm Packages Pose as PostCSS Tools to Deliver Windows RAT
item.description:
Cybersecurity researchers have discovered a set of malicious npm packages that are designed to deliver a Windows-based remote access trojan (RAT).
The list of identified packages, is below -
aes-decode-runner-pro (145 downloads) postcss-minify-selector (256 downloads) postcss-minify-selector-parser (615 downloads)
All the packages were published over the past month by an npm user named
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Five Eyes Group Issues Urgent Call to Tackle Frontier AI Threats
item.description: The Five Eyes Alliance has published a rare call to action for organizations facing AI threats

item.title: The Architecture of an AI-Powered Breach: The Shadow Supply Chain | UpGuard
item.description: Decode three real-world AI breaches. Learn how Shadow AI pipelines bypass security and how real-time browser guidance can stop data leaks.

item.title: Decoding the Copilot Ecosystem | UpGuard
item.description: Don't let Copilot extend its permission sprawl. Learn the structural risks of M365 and GitHub Copilot, and how to enforce real-time browser guardrails.

item.title: Governing Excessive Agency in the Anthropic Ecosystem | UpGuard
item.description: Agentic AI is moving fast. Learn how to govern Claude safely, neutralize Excessive Agency, and enforce real-time browser-level guardrails.

item.title: The 2026 Enterprise AI Security Index | UpGuard
item.description: Cut through the marketing hype. Uncover the architectural risks, data defaults, and vulnerabilities of leading AI models in our AI Security Index.

item.title: The Ultimate Ransomware Defense Guide (2026) | UpGuard
item.description: Learn how to implement an effective security strategy for minimizing the impact of ransomware attacks.

item.title: WhatsApp VBScript Campaign Uses Fake Documents to Install ManageEngine RMM Tool
item.description:
Direct messages sent via WhatsApp are being used to distribute malicious Visual Basic Script (VBScript) files that lead to the installation of legitimate Remote Monitoring and Management (RMM) software.
Per findings from Kaspersky, the active campaign is targeting users of WhatsApp Desktop and WhatsApp Web across Malaysia, Brazil, India, Mexico, Singapore, the U.K., Spain, Taiwan, Australia,
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: OpenAI Expands Daybreak With GPT-5.5-Cyber to Help Defenders Patch Security Flaws
item.description:
OpenAI on Monday said it's releasing an improved version of its GPT‑5.5‑Cyber model to trusted defenders as part of the Daybreak initiative&nbsp;the artificial intelligence (AI) company announced last month.
Calling GPT‑5.5‑Cyber its "strongest model yet for finding and helping patch software vulnerabilities," OpenAI said the model can "sustain deeper analysis across large codebases" to
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: DifyTap Bugs Let Attackers 'Wiretap' AI Chat Histories
item.description: Four vulnerabilities allow attackers to exploit Dify, a platform for AI application building and management, to silently access and exfiltrate sensitive data.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Culafi

item.title: New Apple Exploit Exposes Millions of iPhones Worldwide, No Software Fix Available
item.description: <p>Researchers disclosed usbliter8, a SecureROM exploit affecting older Apple devices that can bypass boot protections with physical access.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com/article/news-apple-usbliter8-securerom-exploit-june-2026/">New Apple Exploit Exposes Millions of iPhones Worldwide, No Software Fix Available</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com">TechRepublic</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Aminu Abdullahi

item.title: Microsoft Confirms Windows Recycle Bin Bug Affects All Supported Versions
item.description: <p>Microsoft confirmed a Windows June update bug that shows internal Recycle Bin file names during permanent deletion, with a fix planned.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com/article/news-windows-june-update-recycle-bin-file-name-bug/">Microsoft Confirms Windows Recycle Bin Bug Affects All Supported Versions</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com">TechRepublic</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Aminu Abdullahi

item.title: ShapedPlugin WordPress Pro Plugins Backdoored in Supply Chain Attack
item.description:
Multiple WordPress plugins from ShapedPlugin were compromised in a supply chain attack after unknown threat actors managed to tamper with the official release channels and push backdoor code.
"Attackers compromised the vendor's build and distribution pipeline, injecting backdoor code into Pro plugin releases distributed through official licensed update channels," Wordfence said in an analysis
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: 29-Year-Old Squid Proxy Bug 'Squidbleed' Can Leak Cleartext HTTP Requests
item.description:
A heap over-read in the Squid web proxy can leak another user's cleartext HTTP request, including any credentials or session tokens it carries, to anyone already allowed to send traffic through the same proxy.
The bug traces to a 1997 FTP-parsing change and is still live in Squid's default configuration. Researchers at Calif.io&nbsp;disclosed it in June&nbsp;and named it Squidbleed (
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Researchers Detail DifyTap Flaws in Dify That Could Expose AI Chats Across Tenants
item.description:
Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed details of four vulnerabilities in Dify, an open-source agentic workflow platform with more than 146,000 GitHub stars, that could allow attackers to stealthily read artificial intelligence (AI) conversions from other customers' applications without requiring authentication.
The vulnerabilities have been collectively codenamed DifyTap by Zafran Security.
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Crypto Heist Fueled by Elaborate Fake Reputation-Boosting Campaign
item.description: Attackers are using multiple online channels — including GitHub, YouTube, and VirusTotal — to build an illusion of trust to spread a cross-platform clipboard hijacker.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Montalbano

item.title: GentleKiller Framework Disables Victims' Security Software
item.description: ESET details GentleKiller, the EDR-killer framework the Gentlemen ransomware gang gives affiliates

item.title: Top 10 Attack Surface Management Software Solutions in 2026 | UpGuard
item.description: Evaluate your next attack surface management software. Compare the top 10 attack surface management platforms and tools for your stack.
